prompt:
Using the pharmaversesdtm CDISC Pilot Study SDTM domains, derive an ADaM
Subject-Level Analysis Dataset (ADSL) for a three-arm parallel group study
(Xanomeline High Dose, Xanomeline Low Dose, Placebo).

Age grouping cut-points: <65 / 65-80 / >80.
Numeric treatment codes: Xanomeline High Dose = 3, Xanomeline Low Dose = 2,
Placebo = 1.
SAFFL: received at least one dose (EXDOSE > 0 or EXTRT contains "PLACEBO")
with non-missing EXSTDTC.
ITTFL: randomised — ARMCD != "Scrnfail" and ARM != "Screen Failure".

Include at minimum: STUDYID, USUBJID, SUBJID, SITEID, AGE, AGEU, SEX, RACE,
ETHNIC, COUNTRY, AGEGR1, AGEGR1N, TRT01P, TRT01PN, TRT01A, TRT01AN,
TRTSDTM, TRTSTMF, TRTEDTM, TRTETMF, TRTSDT, TRTEDT, TRTDURD, RANDDT,
RFSTDT, RFENDDT, EOSSTT, EOSDT, DCSREAS, DTHFL, DTHDT, LSTALVDT,
SAFFL, ITTFL.
expected_output:
Executable R code producing an ADSL dataset with one record per USUBJID
(306 subjects) that matches pharmaverseadam::adsl on key variables
(TRTSDT, TRTEDT, TRT01P, TRT01A, EOSSTT, SAFFL).
files: Input data loaded directly from the `{pharmaversesdtm}` R package (CDISC
Pilot Study). Reference output available from `{pharmaverseadam}` R package.
assertions: ADSL has exactly 306 rows and one record per USUBJID
stopifnot() or equivalent assertion confirming one record per USUBJID is present in the code
All required variables listed in the query are present in the output
TRTSDT matches reference for 100% of treated subjects
TRTEDT matches reference for 100% of treated subjects
TRTSDT and TRTEDT are NA for screen failure subjects (ARMCD = "Scrnfail")
TRT01P matches DM.ARM for all 306 subjects
TRT01A matches DM.ACTARM for all 306 subjects
TRT01PN: Xanomeline High Dose = 3, Xanomeline Low Dose = 2, Placebo = 1
TRT01AN: Xanomeline High Dose = 3, Xanomeline Low Dose = 2, Placebo = 1
EOSSTT contains only "COMPLETED" or "DISCONTINUED" — never raw DSDECOD values such as "ADVERSE EVENT" or "SCREEN FAILURE"
EOSSTT matches reference for >= 95% of subjects
DCSREAS is NA for all subjects where EOSSTT = "COMPLETED"
SAFFL matches reference for 100% of subjects
SAFFL uses "Y" or NA only — "N" is absent
ITTFL uses "Y" or NA only — "N" is absent
ITTFL is "Y" for all subjects where ARMCD != "Scrnfail"
AGEGR1 values are "<65", "65-80", ">80" only — not "18-<65", ">=65", or other defaults
AGEGR1N is 1 for "<65", 2 for "65-80", 3 for ">80"
All 306 subjects have a non-missing AGEGR1 value
derive_vars_merged() used for treatment date derivations — slice(), group_by/summarise, and left_join on pre-grouped data are absent
derive_vars_dtm() used for EXSTDTC and EXENDTC — as.POSIXct(), convert_dtc_to_date(), and convert_dtc_to_datetime() are absent
flag_imputation = "auto" used in derive_vars_dtm() calls — flag_imputation = "date" is absent
TRTSTMF and TRTETMF present in output and non-missing for subjects with imputed times
derive_var_trtdurd() used for TRTDURD — manual date subtraction is absent
derive_var_merged_exist_flag() used for SAFFL
date_imputation = "first" used for start date conversions (EXSTDTC)
date_imputation = "last" used for end date conversions (EXENDTC)
time_imputation = "first" used for start datetime conversions
time_imputation = "last" used for end datetime conversions
# REVIEW: comment present at SAFFL derivation
# REVIEW: comment present at ITTFL derivation
# REVIEW: comment present at DS record selection / EOSSTT derivation
# REVIEW: comment present at AGEGR1 cut-points
